subject: Public Health
title: The Irish Famine and Typhus
description: A quick win: understand The Irish Famine and Typhus.

hook.text: During Ireland's Great Famine, more people died from typhus than from starvation itself.

buildup.text: Famine weakened immune systems and crowded people into workhouses where lice spread typhus rapidly.

discovery.text: Typhus thrives in overcrowded, unsanitary conditions—earning the name 'jail fever' and 'camp fever.'

twist.text: Doctors treating famine victims died at higher rates than their patients—they caught typhus too.

climax.text: The famine killed or displaced 2.5 million people—disease and poverty intertwined as cause and effect.

punchline.text: Famine doesn't just starve. It opens the door to disease.

quiz.question: What killed more people during the Irish Famine than starvation?
